selected_important_turn.text: Well we don't want to make all the bears sick though haha. Okay so let's try this... one of us gets 2 waters with 1 firewood and 1 food. I think that's as close as we'll get to equal. You can choose.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the concrete deal-opening proposal that shaped the final allocation and effectively handed the opponent the power to pick the bundle. It moves the dialogue from information-sharing into a binding choice and so strongly influenced the outcome.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although framed as fair and cooperative, it gave the other party first/sole choice over bundles and didn't present multiple balanced options or explicitly trade across differing priorities. That allowed the opponent to take the clearly higher-value bundle (2 waters + food) while leaving you mostly firewood — a suboptimal capture of integrative potential.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of a single "you can choose" offer, present 2–3 explicitly balanced options that trade items in ways that reflect both parties' priorities (e.g., Option A: you get 2 waters + 1 food, I get X in compensation; Option B: split waters 1/1 and split food/firewood differently). Or ask the opponent which trade-offs they'd accept and propose a compensating package so both sides get some of their top items.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by creating integrative options and compensating trades)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (explicit, balanced options look and feel fairer)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: moderate increase (slightly more negotiation needed but clearer choices speed agreement)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (demonstrates concern for mutual benefit and reduces winner-take-all feel)

value2reason.Low: It's so hot we won't need much of a fire.
value2reason.Medium: Relaxing always makes snacking more fun, so more food is always eaten
value2reason.High: It is super hot, so we will need to stay hydrated and drink lots of water

value2reason.Low: We're fairly skinny and don't eat much.
value2reason.Medium: My wife gets really cold at night.
value2reason.High: My family wants to do a lot of hiking.

dialogue.text: Hello, what a great time of year to go camping.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Yes I can't wait! So sick of being stuck at home. 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: It will be nice to get out and explore the great outdoors, even if it is super hot!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I agree. What supplies could you use more of?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Water for sure, need to stay extra hydrated.  How about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Haha yup I had a feeling about that one. ;-) Yeah my wife has a bunch of crazy hikes planned so water is a must here too. What else?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Food of course, always snack more when relaxing.  Agree on the hiking.  Out in the woods, we won't have to wear masks!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Well we don't want to make all the bears sick though haha. Okay so let's try this... one of us gets 2 waters with 1 firewood and 1 food. I think that's as close as we'll get to equal. You can choose.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that's a fair split, but I'm a bit concerned about the firewood. Since it's so hot, maybe we can adjust to give me 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood, while you take the remaining 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods. That way, we both get what we need most.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see your point, but I think we can do better. How about this: Option A - I take 2 waters and 1 firewood, you get 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods. Option B - we split the waters 1/1, and split the food and firewood differently. Which trade-off do you prefer?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think Option B could work better for both of us. Let's split the waters 1/1, and then divide the food and firewood so we both get what we need. How about you take 1 water, 2 foods, and 1 firewood, while I take 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewoods? That way, we both get our priorities met.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that works for me. Let me just confirm: you take 1 water, 2 foods, and 1 firewood, while I take 1 water, 1 food, and 2 firewoods. That splits all packages and meets our priorities. Deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
